title: pleopeltis indurata
intro: Pleopeltis indurata, also known as the hard polypody fern, is a type of epiphytic fern species native to the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Pleopeltis indurata include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis indurata include teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be made by drying the plant's leaves and stems, and then steeping them in hot water or applying them topically to the affected area.
